Optical Pre-Design · Phase 1
Calculation details
Equations and parameters used to derive optical requirements from mission inputs
1 · Input parameters
SymbolParameterUnitCurrent value
R Slant range to target m —
L Critical dimension of target (smallest relevant feature — shoulder width for person, body for drone, etc.) m —
Npx Pixels on target required by DRI criterion (Detection=3, Recognition=8, Identification=16) px —
p Pixel pitch of selected sensor µm —
λ Representative wavelength of selected spectral band µm —
2 · Angular requirement
θreq = L / ( R · Npx )
The angle subtended by one pixel on the target. Derived from the smallest resolvable feature L divided by the range R and the number of pixels required to resolve it Npx.
—
3 · Minimum effective focal length
IFOVreq = L / ( R · Npx )   [rad/px]
Instantaneous Field of View required per pixel — same as θreq, expressed per pixel.
—
fmin = Npx · p · R / L
Minimum focal length for the sensor to sample the target correctly. Derived from the Nyquist sampling condition: the sensor IFOV (p/f) must be ≤ IFOVreq. Rearranging: f ≥ Npx·p·R/L.
—
4 · Aperture references
Dref = 1.22 · λ · R · Npx / L
Diffraction-limited aperture reference. From the Rayleigh criterion (first zero of Airy disk): θdiff = 1.22·λ/D. Setting θdiff ≤ θreq and solving for D gives Dref. Not a hard minimum — atmosphere and jitter typically limit resolution before diffraction at these scales.
—
F/#max = 1.17 · p / λ
Maximum F/# to maintain MTF at Nyquist ≥ 0.30 (NTR criterion). From the incoherent diffraction MTF formula: MTF(ν) = (2/π)[arccos(ν) − ν√(1−ν²)], solved at νNyquist/νcutoff = 0.585 for MTF ≈ 0.30. This ensures the optical MTF does not collapse at the sensor's Nyquist frequency.
—
DNTR = fmin / F/#max
Minimum aperture to satisfy the NTR criterion given the required focal length. If D < DNTR the system would be too slow (high F/#) and MTF at Nyquist would fall below 0.30.
—
Dreco = max( Dref , DNTR )
Recommended design aperture — the larger of the two aperture references. This is the starting point for Phase 2 architecture selection.
—
5 · MTF budget note
MTFtotal ≈ MTFoptics · MTFpixel · MTFjitter · MTFatmosphere
Phase 1 computes only the optical geometry (sampling requirement). A full MTF budget — including pixel aperture, platform jitter, and atmospheric turbulence — is required in Phase 3 detailed design. At long ranges the atmosphere and jitter terms typically dominate over diffraction.
